questions.modelAnswer: A Pandas Series is a one-dimensional labeled array capable of holding data of any type (integer, string, float, etc.). Its axis labels are collectively referred to as the index.

questions.questionText: What happens to water when you put it in the freezer?

Write a Python program using matplotlib to create a bar graph to represent the following monthly sales:Months: ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May']Sales: [15000, 18000, 22000, 20000, 25000]Add an appropriate title, x-axis label, and y-axis label to the graph.
questions.modelAnswer:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

months = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May']
sales = [15000, 18000, 22000, 20000, 25000]

# Creating the bar graph
plt.bar(months, sales, color='skyblue')

# Adding labels and title
plt.title('Monthly Sales Performance')
plt.xlabel('Months')
plt.ylabel('Sales in ($)')

# Displaying the graph
plt.show()
An organism is any individual living thing that can function on its own. It can range from a microscopic, single-celled bacterium to complex, multicellular organisms like humans, animals, and trees

questions.questionText:
What will be the output of the following Python code?
import pandas as pd
s1 = pd.Series([10, 20], index=['a', 'b'])
s2 = pd.Series([30, 40], index=['b', 'c'])
print(s1 + s2)
questions.modelAnswer:
a     NaN
b    50.0
c     NaN
dtype: float64

questions.questionText: What is the function of the GROUP BY clause and how to filter groups based on conditions?
questions.relevantKeywords: group, group by, identical column values, filter groups, having clause, aggregation, summary
questions.modelAnswer: The GROUP BY clause is used to group table rows with identical column values into single summary rows. Group conditions are filtered using the HAVING clause, whereas WHERE operates before groups are formed.

studentAnswers.answerText:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Data Setup
quarters = ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3', 'Q4']
target_revenue = [12, 15, 18, 22]
actual_revenue = [10, 16, 17, 25]

# Plotting Target and Actual lines with specified colors and line styles
plt.plot(quarters, target_revenue, color='green', linestyle='--', label='Target')
plt.plot(quarters, actual_revenue, color='blue', linestyle='solid', label='Actual')

# Adding Labels and Title
plt.title('Quarterly Revenue Performance')
plt.xlabel('Quarters')
plt.ylabel('Revenue (in Lakhs)')

# Display Legend in the top left corner
plt.legend(loc='upper left')

# Show the final chart on the screen
plt.show()
studentAnswers.aiFeedback: Excellent! Your Python script correctly sets up the data, configures the required line styles and colors, adds the accurate labels and title, positions the legend in the top-left corner, and successfully displays the chart.

studentAnswers.answerText:
import pandas as pd

# 1. Define the dictionary with student names and marks
student_marks = {
    "Alice": 85,
    "Bob": 92,
    "Charlie": 78,
    "David": 95
}

# 2. Convert the dictionary into a Pandas Series
marks_series = pd.Series(student_marks)

# 3. Display the resulting Series
print(marks_series)
studentAnswers.aiFeedback: Perfect! You correctly defined a dictionary named student_marks, created a Pandas Series from it, and displayed the series.
